data.body:
joe-debug investigation complete — root causes proven, two fixes shipped, rest tracked.

CORRECTION to the original report: the b043/seedy-edema-groan "open + completion-signature"
symptom is NOT missing-event corruption. b043's last event is a legitimate reconciler
STATUS closed→open (Jira-authoritative reopen); the signature persists in the snapshot's
compiled_state. There IS a reopen event (authored by "reconciler"). Separate phenomenon
from the fsck corruption.

CONFIRMED ROOT CAUSES (fishbone; flock is local-only and never covered these cross-machine paths):
- RC1 (SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT, ~2400): the Jira reconciler CI workflow used shallow
  (--depth=1) fetch + `git rebase origin/tickets`. The shallow graft defeats git's
  merge-base, so the rebase re-applies the reconciler's stale pre-compaction worktree
  over a laptop COMPACT commit, resurrecting the source files compaction deleted while
  keeping the SNAPSHOT. Proven by (a) production artifact — commit 51e0a578c is
  single-parent off COMPACT 3afe230a0 and re-adds 8 byte-identical deleted files
  (stale-boundary fingerprint), and (b) controlled experiment: only shallow+rebase
  reproduces; full+merge and full+rebase are clean; shallow+merge fails safe (rc=128).
  Deeper enabler: compaction hard-os.remove()s folded files (compact.py:142) instead
  of the documented `*.retired` rename (invariant I1, docs/concurrency.md:35-36,154).
- RC2a (ORPHAN_EVENT false positives, ~519): compaction folds only KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES,
  but fsck flagged any pre-snapshot event absent from source_event_uuids — so
  REVIEW_RESULT (reducer-ignored) was falsely flagged. Deterministic, single-clone.
- RC2b (ORPHAN_EVENT, genuine, ~11 remaining): a concurrent write on a stale base gets
  an HLC timestamp below a peer's snapshot horizon; a clean merge keeps it as a
  pre-snapshot event that replay silently skips — real data loss. Still surfaced.

SHIPPED NOW:
- Fix #1: reconcile-bridge.yml + reconcile-bridge-canary.yml → full history (fetch-depth: 0,
  no --depth=1) + `git merge --no-edit` (not rebase). Regression guard:
  tests/interfaces/store/test_reconcile_workflow_no_shallow_rebase.py (parametrized over both).
- Fix #3: fsck._check_snapshot exempts non-KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES from ORPHAN_EVENT (symmetric
  with compaction) while still flagging genuine KNOWN-type orphans. Tests:
  tests/interfaces/store/test_fsck_snapshot_checks.py (RED→GREEN + mutation-tested).
- End-to-end replication on the live store: ORPHAN_EVENT 530 → 11 (all remaining are
  genuine KNOWN-type -EDIT.json orphans). SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT still growing until fix #1
  is DEPLOYED (CI still runs the old workflow until this lands).

DEFERRED (tracked in new epic adept-hedge-stain / ace2-f784-4b19-4f22, discovered_from this bug):
- #2 implement the `*.retired` non-destructive compaction (durable class-fix for RC1),
- #4 snapshot-horizon-safe reconciliation (RC2b data loss),
- #5 remediate the already-corrupted live store (drive fsck to zero; gated on #1 deploy).

Fixes not yet committed/landed — pending direction on committing + Gerrit.
